ucs.title: [LLM] APT28 (Forest Blizzard) CVE-2023-23397 Outlook NTLM credential coercion via PidLidReminderFileParameter UNC
ucs.description: Hunts Outlook coercing NTLM authentication to an attacker-controlled UNC path via a malicious calendar/task/email reminder — the zero-click primitive Forest Blizzard / GRU Unit 26165 used against NATO ministries, energy and defense orgs from 2022 onward. Outlook.exe initiating outbound SMB/WebDAV to a non-corporate destination is the smoking gun.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(All_Traffic.dest_ip) as dest_ip values(All_Traffic.dest_port) as dest_port values(All_Traffic.bytes_out) as bytes_out from datamodel=Network_Traffic where (All_Traffic.app="outlook.exe" OR All_Traffic.process="*\\outlook.exe") (All_Traffic.dest_port=445 OR All_Traffic.dest_port=139 OR All_Traffic.dest_port=80 OR All_Traffic.dest_port=443) by All_Traffic.src All_Traffic.user All_Traffic.dest All_Traffic.app host | `drop_dm_object_name("All_Traffic")` | iplocation dest | search NOT (dest_ip=10.0.0.0/8 OR dest_ip=172.16.0.0/12 OR dest_ip=192.168.0.0/16 OR dest_ip=100.64.0.0/10 OR dest_ip=169.254.0.0/16) | where dest_port=445 OR dest_port=139 OR (dest_port IN (80,443) AND like(dest,"%@%")) | `ctime(firstTime)` | `ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
DeviceNetworkEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "outlook.exe"
| where RemotePort in (139, 445)
   or (RemotePort in (80, 443) and RemoteUrl has_any ("\\\\", "webdav", "@SSL"))
| where not(ipv4_is_private(RemoteIP))
| where RemoteIPType !in ("Loopback","LinkLocal","Reserved")
| extend SuspectedCVE = "CVE-2023-23397"
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where FileName =~ "outlook.exe"
    | project DeviceId, OutlookPid = ProcessId, OutlookCmd = ProcessCommandLine
) on $left.InitiatingProcessId == $right.OutlookPid and $left.DeviceId == $right.DeviceId
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, RemoteIP, RemotePort, RemoteUrl, ReportId, SuspectedCVE
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: Outlook.exe should never speak SMB/WebDAV to the public internet under any normal business workflow. APT28 weaponized exactly this primitive in CVE-2023-23397 to harvest Net-NTLMv2 hashes from victims worldwide (Microsoft IR mitigation guidance, March 2023, and CERT-EU 2023-014). Microsoft's CVE-2023-23397 script and subsequent Forest Blizzard reporting both call out outbound 445/139 from Outlook

ucs.title: [LLM] APT28 'GooseEgg' post-exploit of CVE-2022-38028 (Print Spooler driver hijack)
ucs.description: Hunts Forest Blizzard's bespoke GooseEgg launcher (execute.bat / doit.bat / servtask.bat dropped in C:\ProgramData\) tampering with the Print Spooler's JavaScript constraints file to gain SYSTEM, then executing follow-on creds tooling. Microsoft attributed GooseEgg exclusively to Forest Blizzard / GRU 26165 in April 2024.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime values(Processes.process) as process values(Processes.process_name) as child_proc values(Processes.user) as user values(Processes.process_id) as pid from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.parent_process_name="spoolsv.exe" AND (Processes.process_name IN ("cmd.exe","powershell.exe","rundll32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","regsvr32.exe") OR match(Processes.process,"(?i)\\\\ProgramData\\\\(execute|doit|servtask)\.bat") OR match(Processes.process,"(?i)wayzgoose|MPDW-Constraints\.js")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.parent_process Processes.process Processes.process_name host | `drop_dm_object_name("Processes")` | append [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Filesystem where Filesystem.file_path="*\\Windows\\System32\\DriverStore\\FileRepository\\*\\MPDW-Constraints.js" by Filesystem.dest Filesystem.user Filesystem.process_name Filesystem.file_path host | `drop_dm_object_name("Filesystem")`] | `ctime(firstTime)` | `ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let goose_paths = dynamic(["\\ProgramData\\execute.bat","\\ProgramData\\doit.bat","\\ProgramData\\servtask.bat","wayzgoose"]);
let susp_children = dynamic(["cmd.exe","powershell.exe","powershell_ise.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe","wscript.exe","cscript.exe","net.exe","net1.exe","reg.exe"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where InitiatingProcessFileName =~ "spoolsv.exe"
| where FileName in~ (susp_children)
   or ProcessCommandLine has_any (goose_paths)
   or ProcessCommandLine matches regex @"(?i)MPDW-Constraints\.js"
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, InitiatingProcessFileName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine, FolderPath, ReportId
| union (
    DeviceFileEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where (FolderPath has @"\Windows\System32\DriverStore\FileRepository\" and FileName =~ "MPDW-Constraints.js" and ActionType in ("FileCreated","FileModified"))
         or (FolderPath has @"\ProgramData\" and FileName in~ ("execute.bat","doit.bat","servtask.bat"))
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, ActionType, FolderPath, FileName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, ReportId
)
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: GooseEgg is a Forest Blizzard-unique tool — Microsoft's April 2024 'Forest Blizzard uses GooseEgg' threat-intel publication tied this exact filename set (execute.bat, doit.bat, servtask.bat, wayzgoose*.dll) and the MPDW-Constraints.js tampering directly to GRU Unit 26165 going back to at least 2020. Spoolsv.exe spawning cmd/powershell or modifying JavaScript constraints files in DriverStore is ove
